lessons.title: 10 ANATOMY AND PHYSIOLOGY
topics: INTRODUCTION
Anatomy and Physiology is the study of the human body’s structure and function. In Physical
Education and Sport, it helps learners understand how the body moves, responds to exercise and
activities: This topic aims to introduce learners to the fundamental principles of anatomy and physiology,
focusing on their application to physical education and sports. Learners will explore the human
body structure and function, laying the groundwork for further study in exercise science and
sports performance.
General Competences:
Learners should acquire the following general competences:
Problem solving: The application of knowledge to tackle real life situations using various strat-
egies and providing solutions to challenging situations within the environment.

lessons.title: 1.11 SPORTS BIOMECHANICS
topics: Introduction
Sports Biomechanics is the study of how the human body moves during sports and exercise.
It uses ideas from science and physics to help athletes and coaches/sports teachers understand
movements like running, jumping, kicking and throwing.
This knowledge helps improve performance, develop better techniques and prevent injuries. For
activities: as Natural (playing grounds

sand, smooth floor, plastic bottles, carpet)

lessons.title: 12 ENTREPRENEURSHIP IN SPORTS
topics: Introduction:
Entrepreneurship in sports is about using business ideas to create value in the world of sports. It
brings together the love for sports and the skills to start and grow businesses. Sports entrepreneurs
can be involved in many areas, such as organizing sports events, selling sportswear, running
gyms, or creating new sports apps.
activities: TERMINOLOGY
Branding: Creating and promoting a unique identity for a business or product.
Business Model: A plan for generating revenue and sustaining a business
Event management: Planning and executing sports events, such as tournaments or
Innovation: Introducing new or improved products, services, or processes to the sports
industry.
Investment: Funding providing to support a business or project.
Marketing Strategy: A plan for promoting a business or product.
resources:  Example: A sports startup focused only on developing high-quality sports apparel
might grow more successfully than one trying to do too much, such as managing
Sports businesses should prioritize profitability over social impact.”

zunderstanding of concepts (reflecting knowledge of concepts)
